---
title: "MIRAGE Two Trait Extension"
site: workflowr::wflow_site
output: workflowr::wflow_html
---

## Overview

`Mirage_Twotraits` is the project repository for the MIRAGE Two Trait Extension. It extends MIRAGE from one trait to two independent traits, with autism as trait 1 and schizophrenia as trait 2 in the motivating first application.

Project links:

- Repository: <https://github.com/Qishi7/Mirage_Twotraits>
- Website: <https://qishi7.github.io/Mirage_Twotraits/>

The first implementation is a gene-level four-state model:

- `00`: neither trait
- `10`: trait 1 only
- `01`: trait 2 only
- `11`: both traits

State `11` represents gene-level pleiotropy. It does not require the same variants to affect both traits. Conditional on the gene state, the two trait-specific likelihoods are products of two single-trait MIRAGE likelihoods.

## Project Goals

The two-trait extension is designed to support:

- detection of pleiotropic genes,
- marginal gene discovery for each trait,
- cautious cross-trait information sharing when the fitted joint architecture supports positive pleiotropic enrichment,
- log-scale numerical calculations for stability,
- simulation-based validation before any real-data analysis.

## Current Implementation

The current code implements a first R version of the four-state EM model using toy or simulated inputs only. It supports trait-specific annotation parameters, trait-specific relative-risk hyperparameters, four-state posterior responsibilities, posterior probabilities for each target, Bayesian FDR tables, and convergence diagnostics.

## Data Policy

This website and repository must not include real ASD, schizophrenia, WES, dbGaP, SPARK, protected, private, or large data files. All examples and tests use toy or simulated data only.

## Pages

- [Method](method.html): model definition, likelihood, EM updates, posterior probabilities, BFDR, and limitations.
- [Simulation](simulation.html): toy simulation checks actually generated by the current implementation.
- [Implementation](implementation.html): function interface, output objects, tests, and development notes.
- [Notes](notes.html): project log, validation checklist, and remaining limitations.
